sitl_integration.description: Windows with WSL and PX4 SITL at ~/px4_16
sitl_integration.skip_message: SITL prerequisites not met — skipping runtime assertions

evals.name: Payload Type Preservation
evals.description: Heartbeat construction preserves MAVLink wire types via (:) indexing
evals.prompt: I need a MATLAB function that builds a MAVLink HEARTBEAT message identifying my ground station. It should take a dialect object and return the populated message struct. I want to make sure the data types stay correct for the wire format — I've had issues with MATLAB converting fields to double.

assertions.description: Uses (:) indexing on Payload.type assignment
Uses (:) indexing on Payload.autopilot assignment
Uses (:) indexing on Payload.base_mode assignment
createmsg called with dialect as first arg, not mavlinkio
Uses (:) indexing on Payload.custom_mode assignment
createmsg is called with the dialect object, not the mavlinkio object
assertions.expected: In the generated MATLAB code (not explanatory text), createmsg is called as createmsg(dialect, ...) where the first argument is the dialect variable. FAIL if the actual code passes a mavlinkio variable (e.g., createmsg(mavlink, ...)) as the first argument to createmsg.

assertions.description: Uses dialect.enum2num for symbolic constants

evals.name: Manual Heartbeat Connection (Workflow B)
evals.description: Connect to non-broadcasting SITL using sendudpmsg heartbeats
evals.prompt: I'm trying to connect MATLAB to a PX4 SITL running in WSL, but the SITL isn't broadcasting — it just listens and doesn't send anything until it hears from me first. Can you write a script that initiates the connection from the MATLAB side? I need to know when the autopilot is actually connected. The host and port should be variables I can change at the top.

assertions.description: Uses sendudpmsg for pre-discovery heartbeat
Binds GCS to LocalPort via name-value pair
Does not use positional connect syntax (udpin)
Polls listClients in a discovery loop
All payload fields use (:) indexing for type preservation
Does not create a mavlinksub subscriber for HEARTBEAT messages
assertions.expected: In the generated MATLAB code, there is NO call to mavlinksub that subscribes to "HEARTBEAT". The built-in heartbeat subscriber in mavlinkio already populates listClients(). A manual HEARTBEAT subscription is redundant. PASS if the code does not subscribe to HEARTBEAT. FAIL if the code calls mavlinksub with "HEARTBEAT" as the topic argument.

assertions.description: sendudpmsg remote port is not 14550 (14550 is the GCS local port, not the SITL listening port)
assertions.expected: The sendudpmsg call uses a remote port that is NOT 14550. The fourth argument to sendudpmsg must be the SITL listening port (e.g., 18570), not the GCS local port. FAIL if sendudpmsg sends to port 14550 as the remote port.

assertions.description: Does not use hallucinated mavlinkmicroservice

evals.name: Auto-Discovery Connection (Workflow A)
evals.description: Connect when SITL is broadcasting — discover first, then sendmsg back
evals.prompt: Write a MATLAB script that connects to a PX4 SITL that is already broadcasting on port 14550. Launch SITL using: sitl = launchSITL(Broadcast=true) (helper at evals/helpers/launchSITL.m — add that folder to path). Once connected, I want to read ATTITUDE messages from the autopilot. Clean up everything when done including teardownSITL(sitl).

context.setup: UAV Toolbox R2025b installed, WSL with PX4 SITL built at ~/px4_16

assertions.description: Launches SITL with Broadcast=true
Polls listClients for auto-discovery
Creates mavlinkclient handle after discovery
createmsg is called with the dialect object, not the mavlinkio object
assertions.expected: In the generated MATLAB code (not explanatory text), createmsg is called as createmsg(dialect, ...) where the first argument is the dialect variable. FAIL if the actual code passes a mavlinkio variable (e.g., createmsg(mavlink, ...)) as the first argument to createmsg.

assertions.description: Subscribes to messages using mavlinksub with a client handle
Reads messages with latestmsgs
Heartbeat payload uses (:) for type preservation
Does not use sendudpmsg (client is discovered, use sendmsg)
Script runs against live broadcasting PX4 SITL

evals.name: Full Lifecycle with Broadcast SITL
evals.description: End-to-end: launch broadcasting SITL, discover, subscribe, read position, clean up
evals.prompt: I have a PX4 SITL that broadcasts MAVLink. Write a MATLAB script that launches it with launchSITL(Broadcast=true) (helper at evals/helpers/launchSITL.m — add that folder to path), connects as a GCS, and prints the drone's GPS position (GLOBAL_POSITION_INT). Run the script and show me the output. Clean up with teardownSITL(sitl) when done.

context.setup: UAV Toolbox R2025b installed, WSL with PX4 SITL built at ~/px4_16

assertions.description: Uses sendmsg with discovered client (not sendudpmsg)
Subscribes with client-scoped mavlinksub
Payload fields use (:) for wire type preservation
Calls teardownSITL for cleanup
Stops and deletes heartbeat timer
Disconnects the mavlinkio object
Does not use sendudpmsg (broadcast SITL is auto-discovered)
Autopilot discovered and position data received
assertions.expected: MATLAB output confirms autopilot discovered via listClients showing SystemID 1 AND position data is printed (lat/lon or GLOBAL_POSITION_INT fields). NOT a timeout, error, or empty result.

assertions.description: Full lifecycle script runs against live broadcasting PX4 SITL
